dispatcher.spec: S6.1: --norc is a value-less long option that precedes -c without ending option parsing, so -c matches and the marker payload refuses dispatcher-payload.

dispatcher.label: intentional-exit-2

Code dispatcher-selector-unresolved maps to scan_reason_category policy-unresolvable (tables/reason_codes.json), which is the value this row's reason_category records.

dispatcher.source: bash -c 'echo hello'  # doc-lattice check runs elsewhere
dispatcher.label: must-certify
dispatcher.spec: S6.1 last sentence / S6.2: the trailing comment carries the doc-lattice marker in the authored raw text, so D2 batches this source (comments count; a raw match anywhere makes the source reachable). The dispatcher command bash -c 'echo hello' is itself marker-free under S6.2 synthetic-safe provenance because no command word carries the marker, so the argv-wide dispatcher rule does not fire and bash is an ordinary not_candidate. With no marker-bearing command word the source certifies with zero invocations. This pins that a marker-free dispatch argv never triggers the dispatcher rule even when the enclosing source is batched by an inert comment marker.

dispatcher.label: intentional-exit-2
dispatcher.spec: S6.1: source is a plain dispatcher head; the argv word ./doc-lattice-env.sh carries the marker (doc-lattice matches doc[-_.]+lattice in its raw-source segment), so the argv-wide rule refuses dispatcher-payload.

look_alike.label: intentional-exit-2
look_alike.spec: S6.3: doc_lattice matches the direct-marker pattern doc[-_.]+lattice, but its casefolded basename is not in _DOC_LATTICE_HEADS {doc-lattice, doc-lattice.exe} and it is no launcher, so the head refuses marker-head-look-alike, not not_candidate.

look_alike.label: intentional-exit-2
look_alike.spec: S6.3: the basename doc.lattice-wrapper matches doc[-_.]+lattice yet is neither doc-lattice nor a launcher, so the head refuses marker-head-look-alike.

look_alike.label: must-certify
look_alike.spec: S6.3 / head rules: the .exe basename casefolds to doc-lattice.exe, which is in _DOC_LATTICE_HEADS, so the head resolves to doc-lattice and certifies the check invocation rather than refusing as a look-alike.

look_alike.label: must-certify
look_alike.spec: S6.3 / head rules: heads are matched on casefolded basename; DOC-LATTICE casefolds to doc-lattice, which is in _DOC_LATTICE_HEADS, so it resolves as the doc-lattice head and certifies lint instead of refusing as a look-alike.

heredoc_guard.label: intentional-exit-2
heredoc_guard.spec: S3.4: the documented permanent regression; the backslash-newline continuation in the unquoted-heredoc body reassembles $(doc-lattice linear), which mvdan tokenizes literally, so the raw-source guard fires a terminal parser-divergence-guard refusal. Clean-empty certification here is an automatic evaluation failure, hence forbidden_outcome certified-empty.

heredoc_guard.label: must-certify
heredoc_guard.spec: S3.4 / D2 subtlety: the single-quoted delimiter <<'EOF' makes the heredoc body inert data, and the raw-source guard only inspects confirmed unquoted-heredoc spans. D2 gates on raw text, and the raw text does still contain the marker, but it lives in an inert quoted-heredoc body that is never a command word, so the guard does not fire and no invocation is produced. The correct tuple is certified with zero invocations, not intentional-exit-2.

heredoc_guard.label: must-certify
heredoc_guard.spec: S3.2 traverse rule: an unquoted-heredoc body with no backslash-newline continuation is a real command-substitution execution source, so the guard stays silent and doc-lattice check certifies.

malformed_tail.label: intentional-exit-2
malformed_tail.spec: S3.1 / S5.3: statement 1 doc-lattice check is a completed top-level statement and is retained; the trailing echo "$( is an unterminated command substitution whose parse error emits exactly one terminal syntax-error refusal. The retained finding is still reported and exit 2 is forced. Family mirror of the stmtsseq canonical.

malformed_tail.label: intentional-exit-2
malformed_tail.spec: S3.1: per S3.1, doc-lattice linear && ( is one incomplete top-level statement (a BinaryCmd whose right arm never parses), so StmtsSeq yields nothing before the error and no evidence is retained; contrast with the semicolon-closed canonical fixture, where the completed left statement is retained ahead of its own unterminated tail.

malformed_tail.label: intentional-exit-2
malformed_tail.spec: S3.1 / S5.3: doc-lattice reconcile --dry-run is a completed statement retained as [reconcile, dry_run=true]; a bare do at statement start is a reserved word where a command is expected. mvdan.cc/sh mirrors bash, which reports 'syntax error near unexpected token do', so the parser raises a terminal ParseError rather than accepting a command named do. The tail is therefore syntax-error, not the command-local control-flow-keyword code, which would require a certified command word that never exists here.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S9 gate 8 (multibyte text): statement 1 echo "[emoji]" certifies; statement 2's first word $X has text=None so it refuses unstable-first-word. The emoji is one Python character, so the refusal is at raw character index 10: e0 c1 h2 o3 space4 quote5 emoji6 quote7 semicolon8 space9 dollar10. The UTF-8 byte offset would be 13; the character-index oracle is what D4 uses.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S9 gate 8 (multibyte value, amended 2026-07-21 dynamic-value rule): statement 2 VAR="naive $X" doc-lattice check carries a dynamic assignment value ($X) and refuses assignment-prefix at the earliest assignment while the argv doc-lattice check stays resolved. The accented e is one character, so VAR begins at raw character index 13: e0 c1 h2 o3 space4 quote5 c6 a7 f8 e-acute9 quote10 semicolon11 space12 V13. The UTF-8 byte offset would be 14. Revision (2026-07-21): the literal-assignment-prefix-with-dynamic-refusal ratification (amendment B) now certifies an all-literal VAR="naive" prefix, obsoleting this fixture's prior all-literal form as a refusal case; the assignment value was changed to "naive $X" so the fixture keeps exercising the dynamic-assignment-prefix refusal this offset oracle tests, with the raw-index arithmetic unchanged. This fixture pins the retain-argv half of the amended S5.2 dynamic rule: the assignment-prefix refusal at raw index 13 AND the resolved check invocation are both required, so expected_invocations is [["check", false]] rather than empty; certifying the argv while refusing the dynamic prefix is what keeps a definite finding rather than dropping the source.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S6.1 / S9 gate 8 (before {0}): the authored shell template is bash $PRE {0} doc-lattice. D2 batches it because the trailing operand word doc-lattice carries the marker in its authored raw-source segment; the {0} sentinel content is excluded from marker provenance (S6.2), so the operand is what makes the command marker-bearing (mirroring the frozen bash-dynamic-selector fixture bash $OPT 'doc-lattice lint'). bash is a recognized shell dispatcher head; the selector $PRE is dynamic selector syntax that is not statically resolvable, so per the dispatcher grammar (tables/dispatcher_grammar.json c_option_grammar.value_options_before_c) the command refuses dispatcher-selector-unresolved rather than silently missing -c. The refusal anchors at the selector word $PRE, which precedes {0} and uses the identity projection, mapping to raw character index 5: b0 a1 s2 h3 space4 dollar5 (P6 R7 E8 space9 brace10 zero11 brace12 space13 d14). Code dispatcher-selector-unresolved maps to scan_reason_category policy-unresolvable (tables/reason_codes.json), which is the value this row's reason_category records. Redesigned in the second adversarial-review round: the prior source bash $PRE {0} carried no authored marker and was unreachable under D2, so the trailing marker operand was appended to make it genuinely batched with the $PRE refusal index unchanged.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S6.5 / S9 gate 8 (inside {0}): the authored template is doc-lattice {0}. D2 batches it because the head word doc-lattice carries the marker in its authored raw text. The head resolves as the doc-lattice executable (launcher_policy _DOC_LATTICE_HEADS), and the payload word is the run body that {0} represents; after substitution that word is the sentinel __doc_lattice_script__, an unrecognized subcommand (not in _SUBCOMMANDS), so launcher-policy resolution refuses it policy-unresolvable at the subcommand word (launcher_policy.py:_resolve_subcommand returns _refused for an unknown subcommand). The refusal span lands inside the substituted sentinel; per S5.2 an offset inside a synthetic sentinel maps to the corresponding {0} start, so the raw index is 12, the brace of {0}: d0 o1 c2 dash3 l4 a5 t6 t7 i8 c9 e10 space11 brace12 zero13 brace14. The sentinel's own text is excluded from marker provenance (S6.2), so the head marker, not the sentinel, is what batches the source. Redesigned in the second adversarial-review round: the prior source bash -c {0} carried no authored marker and was unreachable under D2; this construction refuses genuinely inside the sentinel region and projects to the {0} start.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S9 gate 8 (after {0}): the authored template is bash -e {0} $EXTRA # doc-lattice; {0} substitutes the run-body sentinel. D2 batches the source because the trailing comment carries the doc-lattice marker in the authored raw text (comments count). bash -e has no -c, so bash is not a recognized dispatcher and the command is an ordinary non-candidate; the pre-policy precheck (S5.2 multi-cardinality-word) still runs over every command word, and the trailing unquoted expansion $EXTRA has single=False, so it refuses splitting-unsafe-word at that word. The refusal lies in the run-body region after the sentinel and uses the identity projection, mapping to raw character index 12: b0 a1 s2 h3 space4 dash5 e6 space7 brace8 zero9 brace10 space11 dollar12 (E13 X14 T15 R16 A17 space18 hash19). Redesigned in the second adversarial-review round: the prior source bash -e {0} $EXTRA carried no authored marker and was unreachable under D2, so an inert trailing comment marker was appended to make it genuinely batched with the $EXTRA refusal index unchanged.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S9 gate 8 (parse error): the terminal syntax-error for the unterminated command substitution anchors at its opening $( token. The prefix doc-lattice check; echo " is 25 characters, so $ sits at raw character index 25. The site check is retained per S3.1; this row pins the refusal offset.

offset_oracle.label: intentional-exit-2
offset_oracle.spec: S5.2 / S9 gate 8 (nested sites): inside the command substitution, doc-lattice check certifies as a nested site, and the right operand of && is $Y whose text=None refuses unstable-first-word. The projection preserves the nested offset, mapping to raw character index 29 (the $ of $Y) in echo "$(doc-lattice check && $Y)".

stmtsseq.label: intentional-exit-2
stmtsseq.spec: S3.1: the canonical StmtsSeq drain-and-deduplicate fixture. Statement 1 certifies a site (check retained); the unterminated tail yields the first non-nil error, the co-yielded statement is discarded, the iterator is fully drained without accepting later statements, and duplicate terminal errors are deduplicated so exactly one terminal syntax-error is emitted at the first error position. Hard pin-upgrade tripwire: any parser pin change must re-verify this before the pin lands.

encoder_composition.label: outside-direct-marker-contract
encoder_composition.spec: S4.2 / S5.1: the max-length four-byte-character source (1,048,576 astral characters = 4,194,304 UTF-8 bytes) pins the encoder composition. With ensure_ascii=False the canonical request encodes to 4,194,359 bytes (4,194,360 with the boundary fixture's trailing newline), under the 8,388,608 aggregate cap, so the per-source and aggregate byte caps compose; default ASCII escaping would break the four-bytes-per-character worst case. Per S5.1 the all-astral source is marker-free, so it stays outside the direct-invocation contract: not_applicable with no invocation, not a must-certify scan. Round-3 reclassification: this is a valid at-limit input, not a must-reject one, and moved from protocol/negative/ to protocol/boundary/ accordingly; decoders must ACCEPT it.
